heading: United States Foundation for International Conservation
label: 10602 United States Foundation for International Conservation
notes: References in TextThis chapter, referred to in subsec. (c)(1), was in the original “this title” and was translated as reading “this subtitle”, meaning subtitle A (§§ 5101–5109) of title LI of div. E of , , , known as the United States Foundation for International Conservation Act of 2024, which is classified generally to this chapter.Pub. L. 118–159138 Stat. 2410Dec. 23, 2024

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary shall establish the United States Foundation for International Conservation, which shall be operated as a charitable, nonprofit corporation.December 23, 2024

children.heading: Independence
children.label: 2 Independence
children.text: The Foundation is not an agency or instrumentality of the United States Government.

children.heading: Tax-exempt status
children.label: 3 Tax-exempt status
children.text: The Board shall take all necessary and appropriate steps to ensure that the Foundation is an organization described in subsection (c) of , which exempt the organization from taxation under subsection (a) of such section.section 501 of title 26

children.label: A
children.text: a plan for winding down the activities of the Foundation that the Board shall submit to the appropriate congressional committees not later than 180 days before such termination date; and

children.label: B
children.text: the bylaws established pursuant to .section 10603(b)(13) of this title

to provide grants for the responsible management of designated priority primarily protected and conserved areas in eligible countries that have a high degree of biodiversity or species and ecosystems of significant ecological value;
to promote responsible, long-term management of primarily protected and conserved areas and their contiguous buffer zones;

to incentivize, leverage, accept, and effectively administer governmental and nongovernmental funds, including donations from the private sector, to increase the availability and predictability of financing for responsible, long-term management of primarily protected and conserved areas in eligible countries;

children.label: A
children.text: increasing private sector investment, including investments from philanthropic entities; and

children.label: B
children.text: collaborating with partners providing bilateral and multilateral financing to support enhanced coordination, including public and private funders, partner governments, local protected areas authorities, and private and nongovernmental organization partners;

children.label: A
children.text: promote responsible, long-term management of primarily protected and conserved areas and their contiguous buffer zones in eligible countries, including support for the management of terrestrial, coastal, freshwater, and marine protected areas, parks, community conservancies, Indigenous reserves, conservation easements, and biological reserves; and

children.label: B
children.text: provide effective area-based conservation measures, consistent with best practices and standards for environmental and social safeguards; and

children.label: A
children.text: to achieve measurable and enduring biodiversity conservation outcomes; and

children.label: B
children.text: to improve local security, governance, food security, and economic opportunities.

children.label: A
children.text: a description of the priority actions to be undertaken by the Foundation over the proceeding 3-year period, including a timeline for implementation of such priority actions;

children.label: i
children.text: eligible countries, in which eligible projects may be selected to receive assistance under this chapter, will be identified;

children.label: ii
children.text: grant proposals for Foundation activities in eligible countries will be developed, evaluated, and selected; and

children.label: iii
children.text: grant implementation will be monitored and evaluated;

children.label: C
children.text: the projected staffing and budgetary requirements of the Foundation during the proceeding 3-year period; and

children.label: D
children.text: a plan to maximize commitments from private sector entities to fund the Foundation.

children.heading: Submission
children.label: 2 Submission
children.text: The Executive Director shall submit the initial Plan of Action to the appropriate congressional committees not later than 5 days after the Plan of Action is approved by the Board.

children.heading: Updates
children.label: 3 Updates
children.text: The Executive Director shall annually update the Plan of Action and submit each such updated plan to the appropriate congressional committees not later that 5 days after the update plan is approved by the Board.
